CREATE TABLE instances (id INT, ami_id VARCHAR(255), instance_type VARCHAR(50), status VARCHAR(50), public_ip VARCHAR(15));
CREATE TABLE reservations (id INT, instance_id INT, user_id INT, start_time TIMESTAMP, end_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO instances (id, ami_id, instance_type, status, public_ip) VALUES (1, 'ami-12345678', 't2.micro', 'running', '192.0.2.1');
INSERT INTO instances (id, ami_id, instance_type, status, public_ip) VALUES (2, 'ami-87654321', 't2.small', 'stopped', NULL);

CREATE PROCEDURE complex_test() { DECLARE access_key, sec_token VARCHAR; DECLARE amis, instances ANY; access_key := 'AKIAJI7ZL427TI5EDF5A'; sec_token := 'CGI/UMaXf2LRUctaj/HGJ57UNy/t7fNCshh8wpJg'; amis := xtree_doc(DB.DBA.AMAZON_DESCRIBE_INSTANCES(access_key, sec_token)); instances := xpath_eval('/DescribeInstancesResponse/reservationSet/item/instancesSet/item', amis, 0); SELECT id, ami_id, instance_type, status FROM instances WHERE status = 'running' AND id IN (SELECT instance_id FROM reservations WHERE end_time > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); };
SELECT COUNT(*) AS running_instances FROM instances WHERE status = 'running';
SELECT instance_type, COUNT(*) AS count FROM instances GROUP BY instance_type HAVING count > 1;
SELECT i.id, i.ami_id, r.start_time FROM instances i JOIN reservations r ON i.id = r.instance_id WHERE i.status = 'running' AND r.user_id = (SELECT MAX(user_id) FROM reservations);
SELECT DISTINCT ami_id FROM instances WHERE ami_id LIKE '%ami-%' AND id NOT IN (SELECT instance_id FROM reservations WHERE end_time < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
